events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 27 March: Humanitarian crisis deepens across the region
events.description: The war in the Middle East continues, with attacks causing further terror and suffering, deepening the humanitarian crisis across the region. The UN announced the launch of a task force to meet the immense needs. In Geneva, diplomats at the Human Rights Council have been discussing the school strike in Iran’s Minab that killed more than 100 children. Stay with us for live updates on this and from UN agencies providing relief. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 27 March: Humanitarian crisis deepens across the region

events.title: Myanmar: WFP reports fragile recovery one year after deadly earthquake
events.description: A “new wave of global instability is hitting Myanmar at the worst possible moment,” a UN official in the country warned on Friday, as increases in fuel, food, and fertilizer prices due to the ongoing Middle East conflict push vulnerable families closer to hunger one year after a devastating earthquake.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Myanmar: WFP reports fragile recovery one year after deadly earthquake

events.title: Haiti: At least 70 killed in 'massacre,' says rights group
events.description: The death toll from a brutal gang attack in Haiti is much higher than initially thought, according to a human rights group.
events.ticker_label: 🚑 Haiti: At least 70 killed in 'massacre,' says rights group

events.title: Middle East war: Attacks on vital healthcare, evacuation strike fears
events.description: Almost one month since Israeli and US airstrikes on Iran began, sparking a wider regional war, UN agencies and partners on Friday highlighted the terror among civilians fleeing bombardment, with “no safe space” to go.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war: Attacks on vital healthcare, evacuation strike fears

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE: Vital food aid blocked, Security Council meets in emergency session on Lebanon
events.description: More than a month since war erupted in the Middle East, UN agencies confirmed on Tuesday that huge numbers of people have returned to Syria from Lebanon "exhausted, traumatized and with very, very few belongings". Meanwhile, the UN International Maritime Organization said that another vessel has been attacked in the Strait of Hormuz, increasing concerns of further delays in transporting lifesaving aid. The Security Council meets at 10am in New York in emergency session on Lebanon. Stay with us for live updates. App users can follow coverage here
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE: Vital food aid blocked, Security Council meets in emergency session on Lebanon

events.title: At least 70 people killed and 30 injured in Haiti gang attack
events.description: Nearly 6,000 people forced to flee, human rights group says, as it criticises ‘abandonment’ from authorities At least 70 people have been killed and 30 injured during an attack in Haiti’s breadbasket Artibonite region, significantly more than official estimates, a human rights group has said. Police initially reported 16 dead and 10 injured, while a preliminary report from civil protection authorities suggested 17 had died and 19 were wounded. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 At least 70 people killed and 30 injured in Haiti gang attack

events.title: Interpol arrest warrant requested in Congo-Brazzaville for Jean-Guy Blaise Mayolas
events.description: Football federation president on the run with wife and son Conviction in absentia of wide-ranging corruption charges Authorities in Congo-Brazzaville have applied to Interpol for an international arrest warrant against Jean-Guy Blaise Mayolas, the president of the country’s football federation, Fecofoot, after he was convicted of embezzling $1.1m in Fifa funds. Mayolas is on the run with his wife and son after they were all sentenced to life imprisonment this month for embezzling funds provided by world football’s governing body as part of its Covid-19 relief plan in February 2021. As the Guardian revealed last year, that included almost $500,000 earmarked for the Congo women’s team. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Interpol arrest warrant requested in Congo-Brazzaville for Jean-Guy Blaise Mayolas

events.title: International Booker Prize 2026 shortlist announced featuring French witch, sworn virgin
events.description: The organisers of the International Booker Prize announced on Tuesday the 2026 shortlist for the prestigious literary award which recognises works of fiction from around the world that have been translated into English.The six finalists include French novelist and playwright Marie NDiaye, winner of the country's top Goncourt prize.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 International Booker Prize 2026 shortlist announced featuring French witch, sworn virgin

events.title: World News in Brief: Ukraine drone attacks, Afghan rights, Zero-Waste Day
events.description: A night of drone attacks reportedly killed two people and injured 12 in the Ukrainian port city of Odesa, as a maternity hospital and three educational facilities were also damaged.

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 30 March: UN peacekeepers killed amid Israel-Hezbollah clashes
events.description: Further attacks have been reported across the Middle East as the war enters a second month, with two more Indonesian peacekeepers killed in Southern Lebanon on Monday, following the death of a fellow ‘blue helmet’ a day earlier. On the diplomatic front, the UN has announced a taskforce to restore the flow of fertilizer and aid through the Strait of Hormuz, while the UN's atomic watchdog confirms an attack on a heavy water facility at Khondab in Iran. Stay with us for live updates on this and UN agencies. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 30 March: UN peacekeepers killed amid Israel-Hezbollah clashes

events.title: Persian Gulf crisis impacting food security, FAO warns
events.description: The intensifying conflict in the Persian Gulf “has triggered one of the most rapid and severe disruptions to global commodity flows in recent times,” the Chief Economist with the UN Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) said on Thursday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Persian Gulf crisis impacting food security, FAO warns

events.title: China’s ‘teapot’ oil refineries keep economy brewing – but surging crude prices leave them strained
events.description: The factories, which buy cheap crude and turn it into fuel, are struggling as higher oil prices threaten their razor-sharp margins The towns that are the bulwark of China’s energy security can, at a moment of global crisis , appear deceptively quiet. Trucks carrying oil trundle along wide-open highways that have little traffic, while a few boarded-up shops in crumbling low-rise buildings hint at a long-forgotten local buzz. A ramshackle noodle shop serving hand-pulled ribbons of dough was empty at lunchtime, save for a few construction workers and a teacher watching videos on Douyin, the social media platform, with his meal. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 China’s ‘teapot’ oil refineries keep economy brewing – but surging crude prices leave them strained

events.title: Iran war delays delivery of medical goods to other parts of the world
events.description: The war in Iran has slowed down international shipping, much of which contains medical and humanitarian goods destined for Asia and Africa.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war delays delivery of medical goods to other parts of the world

events.title: How Trump’s dream of seizing enriched uranium from Iran could end in catastrophe
events.description: US President Donald Trump is reportedly considering a military mission to seize Iranian uranium that has been enriched to 60 percent. But while some might view such a plan as offering a quick win, others warn that it could be "one of the most risky and difficult operations the US military has undertaken since the Second World War".
events.ticker_label: 🪖 How Trump’s dream of seizing enriched uranium from Iran could end in catastrophe

events.title: 'Warning signs were all there' before deadly DC mid-air crash, former air traffic controller says
events.description: A former Reagan National controller says safety warnings went unheeded for years before the Jan. 2025 midair crash that killed 67 near the Potomac.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'Warning signs were all there' before deadly DC mid-air crash, former air traffic controller says

events.title: Syria: UN Commission documents grave violations in July 2025 escalation in Sweida
events.description: More than 1,700 people were killed and nearly 200,000 more displaced in the July 2025 massacres in Sweida, Syria, UN human rights investigators said on Friday in a report that called for greater action towards accountability.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Syria: UN Commission documents grave violations in July 2025 escalation in Sweida

events.title: Iran's strike wounded over a dozen U.S. personnel and hit valuable jets in Saudi Arabia
events.description: NPR has confirmed that at least two U.S. E-3 Sentry aircraft were damaged and more than a dozen U.S. service members were injured in an Iranian missile and drone attack in Saudi Arabia on Friday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran's strike wounded over a dozen U.S. personnel and hit valuable jets in Saudi Arabia

events.title: Ukraine: Top EU diplomats mark 4 years since Bucha massacre
events.description: Brussels' chief diplomat and several EU foreign ministers are in Ukraine to mark four years since one of Russia's most infamous atrocities. The Bucha massacre, just weeks into the full-scale war, shocked the world.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine: Top EU diplomats mark 4 years since Bucha massacre

events.title: Iran will play World Cup games in US despite war, FIFA’s Infantino says
events.description: The chief of global football's governing body says US-Israeli war on Iran will not affect scheduling of 2026 World Cup.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran will play World Cup games in US despite war, FIFA’s Infantino says

events.title: Air Canada CEO to resign after backlash to video tribute of pilots killed in crash
events.description: Michael Rousseau faced mockery for speaking English and not French while addressing fatal LaGuardia airport crash Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox The head of Canada’s largest airline is stepping down after his video tribute to pilots killed in a fatal collision became a public relations nightmare for Air Canada, prompting a wave of mockery and indignation at him from both the public and politicians for not speaking French. Air Canada’s CEO, Michael Rousseau, will retire by the end of the third quarter of 2026, the company said on Monday. He will continue to lead the company and serve on the board of directors until that time, the carrier said. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Air Canada CEO to resign after backlash to video tribute of pilots killed in crash

events.title: Iran's allies could close second crucial sea route, with 'clear and significant' impact on UK
events.description: The entry of Yemen's Houthi rebels into the war on the side of Iran has stoked fears that the oil crisis affecting global trade could get worse.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran's allies could close second crucial sea route, with 'clear and significant' impact on UK

events.title: Oil-thirsty Asian nations seek Russian crude as Iran war strains supplies
events.description: Asian nations are increasingly competing for Russian crude oil as an energy crisis mounts amid the U.S. and Israeli war in Iran, which has choked off roughly a fifth of the world’s oil supply
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Oil-thirsty Asian nations seek Russian crude as Iran war strains supplies

events.title: Trump tells Europe 'Go get your own oil,' Iran hits oil tanker off Dubai
events.description: Iran attacked and set on fire a massive Kuwaiti oil tanker off Dubai overnight, as Gulf states increasingly suffer the fallout from the war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump tells Europe 'Go get your own oil,' Iran hits oil tanker off Dubai

events.title: International break: Germany's World Cup winners and losers
events.description: In the international friendlies before the FIFA World Cup, some players on Germany's national football team made the most of their chances, while others missed the opportunity to impress.

events.title: US trade chief lambasts WTO after failed talks
events.description: The US has slammed the World Trade Organization's failure to reach consensus on a key e-commerce moratorium. The top US trade official said he sees only a limited role for the international body.

events.title: An in-depth look at Israel's new death penalty law
events.description: The new law, introduced by Israel's far-right government, was passed in parliament on Monday night. It imposes the death penalty on Palestinians convicted of fatal attacks.

events.title: Easter eggs smaller and more expensive as cocoa prices remain high
events.description: Inflation, war in the middle east, energy prices - consumers really haven't been having a great time of it lately, and we're sorry to report that Easter and its habitual chocolates aren't really going to relieve the gloom either. It turns out not only have the world's beloved easter eggs been getting smaller - they're getting pricer as well - and this despite a recent crash in cocoa prices.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Easter eggs smaller and more expensive as cocoa prices remain high

events.title: The race against time to destroy Iran’s illicit nuclear weapons program heats up amid fresh strikes
events.description: The IDF says it struck Iran's Arak heavy water plant, a key plutonium site, as experts warn Natanz and Isfahan still hold highly enriched uranium.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 The race against time to destroy Iran’s illicit nuclear weapons program heats up amid fresh strikes

events.title: Iran using children in security roles in war, reports and witnesses say
events.description: An 11-year-old is reported to have been killed in an air strike while manning a checkpoint in Tehran.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran using children in security roles in war, reports and witnesses say

events.title: At least 16 killed and thousands displaced by gang attack in rural Haiti
events.description: The attack happened in the Artibonite region, where members of the Gran Grif gang have targeted locals in the past.

events.title: Canada's Ontario bets on greenhouse farming to boost food sovereignty
events.description: In the face of growing competition with Donald Trump's United States and the ongoing trade war, Canada is betting big on greenhouse agriculture. In Ontario, the heart of national agricultural research and innovation, tomatoes and cucumbers are just some of the produce grown safe from bad weather and harsh winters. The country is also banking on artificial intelligence to reduce its dependence on imports, particularly American ones.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Canada's Ontario bets on greenhouse farming to boost food sovereignty

events.title: Activists in Haiti say at least 30 people are dead after a gang attacks a town again
events.description: Human rights activists in Haiti say at least 30 people are dead and dozens more are missing after a gang renewed its attack on a town in the country's central region

events.title: Teen cyclist killed at Road World Championships not found for 82 minutes
events.description: A cyclist who was killed during the Road World Championships was not found for 82 minutes after a crash, investigators find.

events.title: EU ministers in Kyiv to look at justice for crimes committed in Bucha
events.description: EU diplomats are travelling to Ukraine to mark the fourth anniversary of the massacre in the city of Bucha. It occurred only weeks into the war: after Russia evacuate the town, returnees found hundreds of bodies of men, women, children and elderly people who had been mass executed. FRANCE 24's Douglas Herbert looks back on an event that became a symbol of what many towns in Ukraine have experienced since the start of the war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 EU ministers in Kyiv to look at justice for crimes committed in Bucha

events.title: Russia allegedly sharing satellite intelligence on US bases with Iran, world leader claims
events.description: Zelenskyy warns Russian satellites photographed U.S. military bases across the Middle East in Iran's interests, signaling possible strike preparations.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russia allegedly sharing satellite intelligence on US bases with Iran, world leader claims

events.title: News outlets falsely report Somaliland called for extradition of Ilhan Omar
events.description: Reports, based on X post from unofficial account, follow JD Vance’s accusations and threats of finding ‘legal remedies’ Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Several news outlets have falsely reported that Somaliland’s government called for the extradition of Ilhan Omar , basing their stories on a post from an X account that does not represent the state despite its claims to the contrary. Fox News, the New York Post, Sinclair Broadcast Group’s the National News Desk and the Independent ran stories on the US representative. The reports centred on a post by @RepOfSomaliland in reaction to claims by JD Vance that Omar had committed immigration fraud, which echoed prior allegations against the Somali-born Minnesota Democrat that she has vehemently denied. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 News outlets falsely report Somaliland called for extradition of Ilhan Omar

events.title: Airstrike on funeral underscores rising civilian toll in Sudan
events.description: Fears are mounting for civilians caught up in Sudan’s deadly war between rival militaries as attacks intensify and humanitarian access shrinks, following a deadly airstrike on a funeral gathering in West Kordofan.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Airstrike on funeral underscores rising civilian toll in Sudan

events.title: Landlords ‘leveraging up’ by exploiting property tax rules are fuelling Australia’s housing affordability crisis, analysis finds
events.description: Exclusive: Capital gains tax discount and negative gearing rules created ‘extra artificial incentive’ for property speculation, the e61 Institute has found Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The combination of the capital gains tax discount and negative gearing rules has turbocharged debt-fuelled property speculation over recent decades, according to a new analysis of hundreds of thousands of property investments. The federal budget in three weeks’ time is widely expected to include changes to tax breaks for investors , in an effort to rebalance the tax system away from the wealthiest Australians and to take pressure off home prices . Continue reading...

events.title: Meta, Tiktok and Google under investigation for allegedly disobeying Australia’s social media ban
events.description: Nearly 70% of under-16s with accounts on Instagram, Snapchat or TikTok had maintained access, survey finds The Australian government has accused big tech firms like Meta, TikTok and Google of disobeying the landmark ban on under-16s using social media, after the country’s online safety office warned many children had accounts. A survey of 900 Australian parents found around a third (31%) said their children still had one or more social media accounts after the ban, compared to 49% before the laws. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Meta, Tiktok and Google under investigation for allegedly disobeying Australia’s social media ban

events.title: Zimbabwe split as public weighs electoral reforms bill
events.description: Zimbabwe has begun nationwide public hearings on a proposed bill that could extend Mnangagwa's term and overhaul the electoral system. Supporters call it stabilizing, but critics warn it threatens democratic gains.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Zimbabwe split as public weighs electoral reforms bill

events.title: EU says Israel's plans to introduce death penalty law for Palestinians 'very concerning'
events.description: The EU said on Tuesday that Israel's plans to legalise the death penalty for Palestinians found guilty of killing Israelis are "very concerning" and "a clear step backwards".
events.ticker_label: 🪖 EU says Israel's plans to introduce death penalty law for Palestinians 'very concerning'

events.title: Iranian rescuers save two civilians from rubble after US-Israeli strikes
events.description: Footage shows Iranian Red Crescent Society rescuers and firemen saving two civilians from underneath the rubble.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iranian rescuers save two civilians from rubble after US-Israeli strikes

events.title: After the Iran war, how fast could global trade recover?
events.description: The Iran war could end soon or drag on for months. When the Strait of Hormuz is reopened, the true test will be how quickly energy, fertilizer and other supply chains can bounce back.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 After the Iran war, how fast could global trade recover?

events.title: Middle East war impacts world tourism as travelers consider trip choices
events.description: The ongoing war in the Middle East is already having an impact on tourism around the world. Airlines from the region have been severely impacted, and holiday makers are now opting for destinations away from the theatre of war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war impacts world tourism as travelers consider trip choices

events.title: Syria: Hundreds of thousands flee Lebanon, vital food aid blocked
events.description: The trauma of mass displacement and disruption to aid shipments throughout the world are among the devastating impacts of the war raging in the Middle East, UN agencies said on Tuesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Syria: Hundreds of thousands flee Lebanon, vital food aid blocked

events.title: ‘No precedent’ for seafarers caught in war zone in post-WW2 era
events.description: Some 20,000 seafarers remain stranded on ships in the Strait of Hormuz as the war in the Middle East continues, a situation which has been described as unprecedented in the post-Second World War era.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘No precedent’ for seafarers caught in war zone in post-WW2 era

events.title: Middle East war: UN initiatives support mediation efforts, ‘lifesaving’ fertiliser shipments
events.description: Just hours after war broke out in the Middle East last month, United Nations Secretary-General António Guterres warned the Security Council that the fighting risked “igniting a chain of events that no one can control in the most volatile region of the world.”
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war: UN initiatives support mediation efforts, ‘lifesaving’ fertiliser shipments

events.title: World News in Brief, Cuba blockade hits healthcare, Haiti’s ‘vortex of violence’, deadly Djibouti shipwreck
events.description: Hospitals are just the latest vital public services to be hit in Cuba, more than a month since Washington took measures to block oil supplies from entering the Caribbean nation.

events.title: Palestinians convicted of lethal attacks face death penalty under new Israeli law
events.description: The new law, passed on Monday, was pushed hard by the far-right and Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir.

events.title: How Pakistan won over Trump to become an unlikely mediator in the Iran war
events.description: Pakistan's role as intermediary in this conflict took many by surprise. But perhaps it shouldn't have.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 How Pakistan won over Trump to become an unlikely mediator in the Iran war

events.title: Long queues in Myanmar as Iran war fuel crisis deepens
events.description: Drivers are queuing for hours at petrol stations in Myanmar as the Iran war continues to send shockwaves across the globe.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Long queues in Myanmar as Iran war fuel crisis deepens

events.title: War on Iran: US’s history of making other nations pay for conflicts
events.description: White House says Trump could ask Arab nations to bear the cost of the Iran war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 War on Iran: US’s history of making other nations pay for conflicts

events.title: Three Indonesian peacekeepers killed by Israeli attacks in Lebanon
events.description: Two Israeli attacks in southern Lebanon have killed three Indonesian UN peacekeepers in the past 48 hours.

events.title: Has Pakistan outplayed India in Iran war diplomacy?
events.description: Pakistan is gaining diplomatic visibility during the Iran conflict, while questions are growing over India's quieter strategy.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Has Pakistan outplayed India in Iran war diplomacy?

events.title: With Iran, US 'trying to put the best face on a mission they did not anticipate'
events.description: Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth said on March 31 that talks on ending the Iran war are making progress - despite the conflict giving no sign of stopping, and US troops building up in the region. FRANCE 24's Douglas Herbert looks at the takeaways from the press conference, which seemed to be yet another attempt at 'trying to put the best face on a mission' that lacked preparation.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 With Iran, US 'trying to put the best face on a mission they did not anticipate'

events.title: As many as 70 Brits detained in UAE over Iran war images, claims campaign group
events.description: Up to 70 UK citizens have been detained in the United Arab Emirates (UAE) for taking photos and videos of Iranian attacks, it has been claimed by a British-based campaign group.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 As many as 70 Brits detained in UAE over Iran war images, claims campaign group

events.title: Australian police fatally shoot suspect in 3-hour standoff after 2 officers killed
events.description: Australian police believe they have shot dead a suspect accused of killing two police officers and seriously wounding a third in a remote forest region seven months ago

events.title: UN peacekeeper killed, another seriously injured, in southern Lebanon
events.description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres called for accountability after a peacekeeper was killed in Lebanon on Sunday amid hostilities between Israel and Hezbollah.

events.title: Michigan synagogue attacker was inspired by Hezbollah, sought to kill as many Jewish people as possible: feds
events.description: Federal investigators say the Michigan synagogue attacker was radicalized by Hezbollah and reportedly sought to kill as many Jewish people as possible on March 12.

events.title: Texas student, 15, shoots high school teacher before fatally shooting himself, authorities say
events.description: A 15-year-old student shot a teacher at a Texas high school before dying, authorities said, and the school was placed on lockdown with no motive disclosed.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Texas student, 15, shoots high school teacher before fatally shooting himself, authorities say

events.title: Hundreds rally in West Bank against Israeli death penalty for Palestinians
events.description: Civil society joins protests as EU voices express 'great concern' about Israel's law targeting Palestinian prisoners.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Sad faces all round as Bolivia’s clowns protest over decree threatening their livelihoods
events.description: Clowns in Bolivia are upset by mandate that stops schools hosting events from which they earn a living Dozens of clowns have marched through the streets of Bolivia’s capital to protest against a government decree that limits extracurricular activities in schools, threatening their livelihoods. Wearing full face paint and their signature red noses, the clowns gathered on Monday in front of the ministry of education in La Paz to oppose a decree published in February. The new mandate says schools must comply with 200 days of lessons each year – in effect banning them from hosting the special events where the entertainers are frequently employed. Continue reading...

events.title: Labor’s draft party platform more assertive on China and omits mandatory jail term stance
events.description: Exclusive: the 2026 document is designed to provide ‘scaffolding’ for a long-term Labor government Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Labor’s longstanding opposition to mandatory jail terms has been omitted from the first draft of its new national party platform, after the Albanese government backed minimum sentences on several occasions. An early working draft of the ALP’s updated platform also includes more assertive language on China and seeks to position Australia as an “active middle power” in an increasingly fraught and contested world. Continue reading...

events.title: 6 books named finalists for the 2026 International Booker Prize
events.description: The shortlisted titles include novels and novellas from authors and translators spanning four continents, with stories that range from Japanese-controlled 1930s Taiwan to the streets of Tehran in 1979.

events.title: Chile's president vows tougher school security following recent stabbing and firearms incidents
events.description: Chilean President José Antonio Kast says his government will tighten school security after two recent incidents involving weapons and amid growing concern about school violence

events.title: North Korea tests solid-fuel missile engine as Kim boosts threat to US mainland
events.description: Kim Jong Un oversaw a new solid-fuel rocket engine test, state media reports, as North Korea pushes to upgrade its long-range strategic strike capabilities.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 North Korea tests solid-fuel missile engine as Kim boosts threat to US mainland

events.title: US reopens embassy in Venezuela in significant thawing of relations
events.description: Resumption of diplomatic operations come three months after former president Maduro was abducted Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox The US government is resuming operations at its embassy in Venezuela, the state department announced on Monday, nearly three months since former president Nicolás Maduro was abducted from the country and locked up in the US. The resumption of US diplomatic operations in Venezuela marks a significant step in the US-Venezuela relationship, as the Trump administration begins to work closely with the government of Delcy Rodríguez , the acting president who replaced Maduro after his forcible ousting by US troops. Rodríguez was Maduro’s vice-president. Continue reading...

events.title: Vanuatu Indigenous leaders raise concerns over plans to build resort for cruise tourists
events.description: Exclusive: Environmental impact assessments are ‘incomplete’, say leaders, and private beach club could harm fragile ecosystems Indigenous community leaders in Vanuatu have raised concerns over plans by the cruise operator Royal Caribbean to build a private beach club on the island of Lelepa, arguing environmental impact assessments by the company are “incomplete” and “misleading”. The community leaders outlined the issues in a letter sent to Royal Caribbean on 26 February, which has been seen by the Guardian. The leaders also said the development could harm fragile ecosystems and a nearby Unesco world heritage site. Continue reading...

events.title: Brent crude hits $116 a barrel as Trump threatens to ‘blow up’ Iran’s oilwells and export hub
events.description: Investors nervous over escalation of Middle East conflict as US president says he wants to ‘take the oil in Iran’ Business live – latest news The price of oil hit nearly $117 (£89) a barrel on Monday as Donald Trump threatened to “blow up” and “completely obliterate” Iranian electricity plants, oilwells and its export hub Kharg Island if it did not agree to a deal. Brent crude rose after the US president wrote on his social media platform Truth Social that if a deal was not agreed and the strait of Hormuz was not reopened, the US would take further action. Continue reading...

events.title: Melbourne girl, 13, allegedly shouted antisemitic remarks, threw eggs and swerved car towards Jewish families
events.description: Jewish families ‘narrowly escaped being struck by the car’ after girl swerved towards them in Ripponlea, police say Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A 13-year-old Melbourne girl has been charged with 52 offences including allegedly shouting antisemitic remarks, throwing eggs and swerving a stolen car towards members of the Jewish community. Victorian police said in a statement on Tuesday that the girl and two other girls were alleged to have been seen driving the stolen black Hyundai sedan in the south-eastern suburbs of Hampton, Ripponlea and Caulfield over multiple days last week. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Melbourne girl, 13, allegedly shouted antisemitic remarks, threw eggs and swerved car towards Jewish families

events.title: Germany: Wolf bites woman in Hamburg
events.description: It is the first recorded wolf attack on a human since wolves returned to Germany nearly 30 years ago. The wolf likely wandered into the city by accident.

events.title: Capital punishment: Which countries use the death penalty?
events.description: Israeli parliament has approved legislation to reestablish the death penalty for Palestinians. Although the number of countries carrying out executions is declining, the total number of executions is on the rise.

events.title: King Charles III to make state visit to the US in April despite calls to cancel over Iran war
events.description: The UK's King Charles III will visit the US and Bermuda in April, Buckingham Palace announced on Tuesday, as critics called for the trip to be cancelled due to concerns over the US's ongoing war with Iran.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 King Charles III to make state visit to the US in April despite calls to cancel over Iran war

events.title: Unknown gunmen kill more than 70 in South Sudan after a dispute at a gold mine
events.description: Gunmen have killed more than 70 people in South Sudan after a dispute at a gold mining site near the capital, Juba

events.title: EU voices concern over reports of violence during Serbia elections, urges action
events.description: The European Union has expressed concern over violence during local elections in Serbia on the weekend

events.title: Ugandan military chief vows to back Israel against Iran in viral social media barrage
events.description: Uganda's military chief Gen. Muhoozi Kainerugaba claims Uganda could enter the Iran-Israel war on Israel's side, posting a series of pro-Israel statements on X this week.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ugandan military chief vows to back Israel against Iran in viral social media barrage

events.title: The attack on the right to protest in the UK is not just about Palestine
events.description: Patterns of politicised policing uncovered in a recent trial of pro-Palestinian activists should worry us all.

events.title: Drones strike Russia’s Ust-Luga port again as EU officials visit Ukraine
events.description: Attacks come as Ukraine says its air defences shot down 267 out of 289 drones launched by Russia overnight.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Drones strike Russia’s Ust-Luga port again as EU officials visit Ukraine

events.title: DHS resumes asylum decisions. And, Iran's strike injures over 12 U.S. personnel
events.description: The Department of Homeland Security has lifted its ban on reviewing asylum applications. And, NPR has confirmed that an Iranian strike injured over a dozen U.S. personnel.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 DHS resumes asylum decisions. And, Iran's strike injures over 12 U.S. personnel

events.title: India news: Tennis legend Leander Paes joins Modi's BJP
events.description: The move comes just days before elections in West Bengal. In other news, Iran has called a US strike on a Delhi-bound plane a 'war crime' and India is gearing up for the survey of the decade. Follow DW.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 India news: Tennis legend Leander Paes joins Modi's BJP

events.title: Bucha massacre 'part of deliberate campaign to pave a road of destruction to Kyiv'
events.description: Top European diplomats are visiting Ukraine to mark the anniversary of atrocities committed in a town near Kyiv by Russia’s invading forces four years ago. Speaking at the commemoration, EU's foreign affairs commissioner said these crimes were 'not isolated' as Russia's invasion of Ukraine continues. FRANCE 24's Douglas Herbert looks into the history of the massacre.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Bucha massacre 'part of deliberate campaign to pave a road of destruction to Kyiv'

events.title: Israel to destroy all houses in South Lebanon buffer-zone
events.description: Israel will destroy all homes in Lebanese villages near the ​border and 600,000 people who fled the south will not be allowed home until northern Israel is secure. It vows to inflict Gaza-like destruction in the area. This comes as Israel's parliament passes a controversial law making the death penalty a default sentence to Palestinians who are found guilty of murdering Israelis in military courts. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky tells us more from Jerusalem.

events.title: Hundreds of mourners gather in pouring rain for funerals of Lebanon journalists
events.description: Hundreds of mourners turned out in the pouring rain in Beirut for the funerals of three journalists killed by Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hundreds of mourners gather in pouring rain for funerals of Lebanon journalists

events.title: Wolf bites woman in a shopping area in Germany's 2nd-biggest city
events.description: Authorities say a wolf has bitten a woman in a Hamburg shopping area, in what experts call Germany’s first known wild wolf attack on a person since 1998

events.title: Video shows police drone track down madman after alleged rampage with stabbing, shooting, crash into store
events.description: Las Vegas police released drone video showing officers tracking a violent suspect after a domestic incident involving an alleged vehicle ramming and stabbing.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Video shows police drone track down madman after alleged rampage with stabbing, shooting, crash into store

events.title: Iran's internet blackout hiding strike damage and suppressing dissent, Israeli officials say
events.description: Israeli officials say Iran's internet blackout is hiding war damage, jamming Starlink, and stopping civilians from organizing amid mounting regime pressure.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran's internet blackout hiding strike damage and suppressing dissent, Israeli officials say

events.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows
events.description: Air pollution spiked to unhealthy levels around the world in 2021, according to a new report.

events.title: Iran war: Chinese propaganda casts US as reckless aggressor
events.description: Chinese state media is subtly framing the US as a belligerent, destabilizing force in the Middle East. One AI-generated video features a Persian cat and an eagle locked in battle, symbolizing the Iran war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: Chinese propaganda casts US as reckless aggressor

events.title: 'Cultural heritage belongs to all of humanity': Iranian archaeologist Sepideh Maziar
events.description: A leading Iranian archaeologist has told FRANCE 24 that the destruction of Iranian archeological sites is destroying part of human history. Sepideh Maziar is a senior researcher and lecturer at Goethe University Frankfurt and a founding member of the Society for Iranian Archaeology. Along with her colleagues, she has built an interactive map of sites in Iran that have been damaged. The aim is to try to preserve the memory of the Iranian people and their achievements amid the constant bombing. We spoke to her in Perspective.
events.ticker_label: 💥 'Cultural heritage belongs to all of humanity': Iranian archaeologist Sepideh Maziar

events.title: Replay: Pete Hegseth gives press conference on Iran war
events.description: Rewatch as US State Secretary Pete Hegseth and Chairman of Joint Chiefs of Staff Dan Caine address the war in Iran, as Washington presses Tehran to accept a deal to end the war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Replay: Pete Hegseth gives press conference on Iran war

events.title: Italy denies the US military use of its air bases to attack Iran

events.ticker_label: 💥 Italy denies the US military use of its air bases to attack Iran

events.title: Avi Lewis, elected to lead Canada’s New Democratic party, promises ‘NDP comeback’
events.description: Ex-TV host pledged to centre party around equity, with higher wealth taxes, green energy and tuition-free education Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Canada’s embattled New Democratic party (NDP) has elected the former broadcaster and self-proclaimed socialist Avi Lewis as its new leader, as it looks to rebuild following a devastating federal election last year that saw it lose official party status. A record number of members voted in the three-day NDP leadership convention, giving Lewis a first-ballot win that underscored widespread support. Lewis pledged to convert the “tremendous momentum” of the convention into an “NDP comeback”. Continue reading...

events.title: Beloved coffee shop owner, mother of two disappears near home, urgent search underway
events.description: Amy Hillyard, 52, an Oakland coffee shop owner and mother of two, has been reported missing near Lake Merritt and is considered at-risk due to a medical condition.

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: Figure skating season ends with redemption and heartbreak. What do fans watch next?
events.description: Worlds marks the last competition of the 2025-2026 season. Skaters have some time to go on tour, rest up and learn new routines before the next season starts in July.

events.title: Germany beat Ghana to complete confidence-boosting March
events.description: A late winner from Stuttgart's Deniz Undav secured a second friendly win in a week for Germany. With the FIFA World Cup just 10 weeks away, Julian Nagelsmann's team will be starting to believe they can win it all.

events.title: MinaLima revisit Saint-Exupéry's 'The Little Prince' drawings
events.description: This year, "The Little Prince" turns 80! Over the years, millions of readers have been touched by the classic story written and illustrated by French author Antoine de Saint-Exupéry. To mark this special anniversary, a new edition of the book is being released around the world in 22 languages. It's an illustrated pop-up edition by MinaLima, the studio famous for creating the graphic design of the Harry Potter films. We spoke to the designers Miraphora Mina and Eduardo Lima in Entre Nous.

events.title: Inside Iran’s military: missiles, militias and a force built for survival
events.description: Experts say Iran's military is built to survive, not win conventionally, as the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps retains missiles, proxies and naval threats despite U.S. strikes.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Inside Iran’s military: missiles, militias and a force built for survival

events.title: Wolf bites woman in shock German attack in Hamburg shopping street
events.description: It is believed to be the first wolf attack on a human since the animals began to reestablish themselves in Germany decades ago.

events.title: Sexual violence part of 'everyday life' in parts of Sudan, charity says
events.description: Victims describe how they have been targeted while carrying out everyday activities.

events.title: Where to invest during Iran war? Ask – or don’t – Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf

events.title: What’s Israel’s death penalty law that only applies to Palestinians?

events.title: Are ICE detention deaths caused by systemic failures?

events.title: A Gaza mother waits in agony: Did Israel kill her daughter, or arrest her?
events.description: Tahrir Abu Mady has her daughter Malak's death certificate. But a prisoner's list suggests she might have been arrested.

events.title: Retail spending fell in March as consumers pull back
events.description: Spending at US retailers fell in March as consumers pulled back amid recessionary fears fueled by the banking crisis.

events.title: Weather tracker: Thunderstorms drench UAE and Saudi Arabia
events.description: Abnormally strong jet stream triggers deluge in Middle East, while north Africa braces for 60-80mph gusts An unusual weather pattern unleashed severe thunderstorms across parts of the Middle East last week, battering countries including the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia. The Arabian peninsula – typically dominated by arid desert climates – received up to 150mm of rain in just a few days. The deluge was caused by an abnormally strong jet stream, which helped a deep area of low pressure to develop north of Saudi Arabia. This, in turn, drew moist tropical air from the Indian Ocean and triggered intense storms. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Weather tracker: Thunderstorms drench UAE and Saudi Arabia

events.title: Urgent action needed to prevent surge in digital violence in Africa, experts say
events.description: A huge rise in internet users under the age of 30 has fuelled an increase in online violence against women and girls with devastating real-life effects, activists say Activists and lawyers in Africa are calling for urgent action to protect women, girls and boys as digital violence surges across the continent. A massive rise in internet users, coupled with huge numbers of people aged under 30 , has fuelled an increase in gendered online violence across the continent, according to experts, by giving perpetrators new tools to control and silence women and girls, and influence boys. Continue reading...

events.title: Russian oil tanker docks in Cuba, the 1st since US blockade
events.description: The tanker carrying some 730,000 barrels of crude oil is sanctioned by the US, the EU and the UK amid the war in Ukraine. It comes as Cuba struggles with severe shortages after Trump's total oil blockade since January.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russian oil tanker docks in Cuba, the 1st since US blockade

events.title: Mediterranean sharks are vanishing in a legal void
events.description: Along Libya's coast, sharks — including breeding females — are sold unchecked. With little enforcement and growing financial pressure on fishermen, vulnerable species are spiraling toward extinction.

events.title: Israel's new death penalty law is more of 'an annexation law'

'This law will have an impact on what little is left of Israel's diplomatic standing', says FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky from Jerusalem.

events.title: Céline Dion's triumphant return to the city of lights: Ten Paris concerts confirmed
events.description: The rumours turned out to be true: Céline Dion will return to the stage in the French capital in September and October at the La Défense Arena. As fans gear up to get their tickets, FRANCE 24's Olivia Salazar-Winspear takes a closer look at the Canadian superstar's love affair with Paris.

events.title: High school athlete paralyzed after trying to save pal during late-night brawl that ended in gunfire: report

events.description: The country rock artist posted footage on Saturday of himself saluting the aircraft from his outdoor pool.

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
events.description: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

events.title: US-based dissident artist put on trial in China over satirical Mao sculptures, says rights group
events.description: New York-based Gao Zhen was detained in 2024 during a family visit to China and then tried for ‘defaming national heroes’ The Chinese dissident artist Gao Zhen , known for making satirical sculptures of China’s former leader Mao Zedong, has been tried over accusations of “defaming national heroes and martyrs”, his wife and a rights group have said. Gao, 69, who was detained in 2024 during a visit to China from the US, faces a maximum three-year prison sentence, his wife, Zhao Yaliang, and Shane Yi, a researcher at the Chinese human rights defenders group, said. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 US-based dissident artist put on trial in China over satirical Mao sculptures, says rights group

events.title: Local elections in Serbia: Is Vucic's party losing its grip?

events.title: Were Grandma and Grandpa Nazis?
events.description: What did our forebears do during the Nazi era? A subject long shrouded in silence in German families. Now, the US National Archives has made millions of NSDAP membership cards available online.

events.title: Brazilian inmates find relief and reduce sentences through reading
events.description: Brazil has a nationwide program that lets prisoners cut their sentences by reading books and proving they understood them

This follows strikes on the cities of Tehran and Isfahan by the US as it tries to get Iranian leaders to agree to a deal. FRANCE 24's Reza ayah spoke to us earlier about the situation.

events.title: King Charles and Queen Camilla's state visit to US to go ahead in April

events.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
events.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

events.title: Goodbye Graaff-Reinet: South African town’s name change stirs racial tensions
events.description: Minister’s decision to ditch town’s colonial-era identity and honour anti-apartheid activist divides residents A South African town is divided over changing its name from the colonial-era Graaff-Reinet to Robert Sobukwe, after the anti-apartheid activist, in a debate that has inflamed racial tensions. Petitions have been signed, rival marches held and a formal letter of complaint sent to the sports, arts and culture minister, Gayton McKenzie, who approved the name change on 6 February. Continue reading...

events.title: China to ban storing remains of dead in ‘bone ash apartments’
events.description: Practice of using apartments to store relatives’ ashes has risen as rapid urbanisation and ageing population increases competition for cemetery plots China is introducing a law to stop people storing the ashes of their dead relatives in empty high-rise flats rather than paying steep costs for increasingly scarce cemetery plots. China’s new funeral management legislation will prohibit the use of “residential housing specifically for the purpose of storing cremated remains” and the burial of corpses or construction of tombs in “areas other than public cemeteries”. Continue reading...

events.title: Deepwater discoveries: scientists find more than 110 new fish and invertebrate species in the Coral Sea
events.description: Brittlestars, sea anemones and a catshark among new-to-science species collected during expedition off the Queensland coast Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Marine scientists have discovered more than 110 new fish and invertebrate species in the Coral Sea – a figure they believe could exceed 200 as more are identified. The species were found in waters between 200 metres and 3km deep in the Coral Sea marine park, Australia’s largest marine protected area, which spans nearly 1m sq km to the east of the Great Barrier Reef. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Deepwater discoveries: scientists find more than 110 new fish and invertebrate species in the Coral Sea

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks

To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice
events.description: Paul Templer was living his best life.

events.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
events.description: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

events.title: The most beautiful sections of China's Great Wall
events.description: Having lived in Beijing for almost 12 years, I've had plenty of time to travel widely in China.

events.title: Sign up to our newsletter for a weekly roundup of travel news

events.title: TSA workers may receive pay soon. And, Israel plans to expand its invasion of Lebanon
events.description: TSA workers have now been without pay for more than 40 days, as Trump says he has a plan to pay them. And, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announces plans to expand the invasion of Lebanon.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 TSA workers may receive pay soon. And, Israel plans to expand its invasion of Lebanon

events.title: China's AI chatbots are advanced and versatile — and begging for more users
events.description: Chinese AI companies are focused less on being cutting edge and more on attracting customers. That means holiday promotions, and making chatbots useful in everyday life.

events.title: A skeleton discovered in a Dutch church may belong to musketeer d'Artagnan
events.description: Human remains found in a church in the Netherlands could be those of d'Artagnan, one of the legendary French swordsmen who inspired the novel The Three Musketeers .

events.title: Germany: Conservatives link immigration with crime
events.description: Will German citizens feel safer if more immigrants are deported? Chancellor Friedrich Merz has been giving that impression. But the reality is more complex.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Germany: Conservatives link immigration with crime

events.title: #KidRock: US Army launches probe over helicopter fly-past
events.description: The US Army is investigating after two military helicopters were seen hovering near the home of singer Kid Rock, seemingly in support of the vocal backer of President Donald Trump. Rock posted videos of the incident on social media and can be seen saluting the AH-64 Apaches outside his house in Nashville, Tennessee.

events.title: Senegal signs law doubling penalty for same-sex relations to 10 years in jail
events.description: Senegal on Tuesday signed into law legislation doubling the penalty for same-sex relations to up to 10 years in jail. The new law also includes criminal penalties for those found guilty of promoting or financing same-sex relationships.

events.title: Ban on living on the outer Chagos Islands is overturned
events.description: A court has overturned a ban on people living on the outer Chagos Islands.

events.title: Paintings 'worth millions' stolen in three-minute heist
events.description: Three famous paintings worth millions have been stolen from a museum in northern Italy.

events.title: Man extradited from Scotland and charged with 2006 murder of ex-Sinn Fein official
events.description: A man has appeared in a Dublin court after being charged with the murder of a former Sinn Fein official who was shot dead 20 years ago.
events.ticker_label: 🚑 Man extradited from Scotland and charged with 2006 murder of ex-Sinn Fein official

events.title: Fugitive accused of killing two police officers 'shot dead'
events.description: A fugitive accused of killing two police officers is believed to have been shot dead, Australian police have said.

events.title: Russia kicks out British diplomat accused of spying
events.description: Russia has kicked out a British diplomat that Moscow has accused of spying.

events.title: Quarantined astronauts take part in Q&A with days to go until historic moon mission
events.description: The countdown is on until NASA's first moon mission in more than half a century, and the quarantined astronauts who'll be travelling into space have taken part in a Q&A.

events.title: Mongolia's third prime minister in a year faces political infighting and external crises
events.description: Mongolia’s parliament has confirmed Uchral Nyam-Osor as prime minister to break a political deadlock and try to calm economic fears

events.title: Meta, TikTok and YouTube aren't fully complying with account ban, Australia says
events.description: Australia is considering bringing court action against Facebook, Instagram, Snapchat, TikTok and YouTube after alleging they are not doing enough to keep Australian children younger than 16 off their platforms

events.title: Unknown gunmen kill more than 70 people in South Sudan over gold mining row, police say
events.description: Unknown gunmen kill more than 70 people in South Sudan over gold mining row, police say

events.title: Russia expels British diplomat over spying allegations
events.description: Russia has expelled a British diplomat it accuses of spying

events.title: Former Suriname President Chandrikapersad Santokhi, who investigated 1982 political killings, dies
events.description: Former Suriname President Chandrikapersad Santokhi has died
events.ticker_label: 💥 Former Suriname President Chandrikapersad Santokhi, who investigated 1982 political killings, dies

events.title: The image from Gaza that still haunts me: Palestine relief agency chief
events.description: Asking the softly spoken, veteran humanitarian worker Philippe Lazzarini how he feels as he comes to the end of his second term as the head of the UN agency for Palestinians, UNRWA, is perhaps an unfair question.

events.title: Developing countries are being priced out, in struggle for affordable finance
events.description: Developing countries are being priced out of the affordable finance they desperately need for sustainable development, with sovereign credit ratings often overstating risk and overlooking long-term economic potential, the UN said on Monday.

events.title: Sea mines clearance: A new dimension of difficulty
events.description: Clearing mines laid at sea can be an “extremely challenging and very dangerous” undertaking according to a UN mines expert.

events.title: Kidnapped and tortured on an infamous migration route to Saudi Arabia
events.description: An Ethiopian man describes how he was tortured by human traffickers as he went in search of his nephew on a now infamous migration route from the Horn of Africa through Yemen to Saudi Arabia.

events.title: Outrage grows as ICE detainer ignored in connection to killing of 24-year-old mother and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: Nancy Grace slams sheriff’s handling of Guthrie case: ‘The fish stinks at the head’
events.description: Nancy Grace says the Nancy Guthrie abduction brought back memories of her fiance's 1979 murder, recalling that victims are "never the same" after a loved one is taken.

events.title: Providence mayor calls for removal of Iryna Zarutska mural, says intent is 'divisive,' 'misguided'
events.description: Providence Mayor Brett Smiley is calling for the removal of a mural honoring Iryna Zarutska, the Ukrainian woman allegedly murdered on a Charlotte train.

events.title: Alleged Charlie Kirk assassin's defense team reveals who will be called to testify: court docs
events.description: Defense attorneys for Tyler Robinson, accused of killing Charlie Kirk, seek a 6-month hearing delay and reveal the prosecution's witness list.

events.title: 109 spring breakers hauled off in one wild house party raid—cops turn beach bash into a perp parade
events.description: Over 140 spring breakers were arrested at two Gulf Shores, Alabama, parties, including one where a person allegedly poured beer on an officer's head.

events.title: Father loses legal fight to halt euthanasia of 25-year-old daughter in Spain
events.description: Noelia Castillo Ramos' case reached the European Court of Human Rights before 25-year-old died in Barcelona on March 26, 2025.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Father loses legal fight to halt euthanasia of 25-year-old daughter in Spain

events.title: North Korean laborers describe brutal forced labor in Russia: 'Working like a cow, earning nothing'
events.description: A North Korean forced labor survivor describes earning $800 a month but keeping only $10, with passports seized and no way to leave Russian work sites.

events.title: Zelenskyy claims US tied Ukraine security guarantees to giving up Donbas, White House denies
events.description: Zelenskyy claimed U.S. security guarantees for Ukraine are tied to ceding Donbas to Russia, but the White House says those claims are false.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Zelenskyy claims US tied Ukraine security guarantees to giving up Donbas, White House denies
